title: World Cup 2026 LIVE: June 26 Scores, Results and Updates

original_text:
1:40 a.m. ET — Round of 32 Taking Shape After Egypt, Cape Verde Results
We’re now getting a clearer picture as to who will face off in the Round of 32. As of Saturday morning, 9 of the 16 matches for the knockout stages are confirmed, with Egypt, Belgium, Paraguay, Sweden, Ivory Coast, and Cape Verde sealing their spot in the next round.
Here’s a look at the confirmed fixtures.
🇿🇦 South Africa vs. 🇨🇦 Canada — June 28, 3 p.m. ET, SoFi Stadium, Inglewood, California (Los Angeles)
🇧🇷 Brazil vs. 🇯🇵 Japan — June 29, 1 p.m. ET, NRG Stadium, Houston, Texas
🇩🇪 Germany vs. 🇵🇾 Paraguay — June 29, 4:30 p.m. ET, Gillette Stadium, Foxborough, Massachusetts (Boston area)
🇳🇱 Netherlands vs. 🇲🇦 Morocco — June 29, 9 p.m. ET, Estadio BBVA, Monterrey, Mexico
🇨🇮 Ivory Coast vs. 🇳🇴 Norway — June 30, 1 p.m. ET, AT&T Stadium, Arlington, Texas
🇫🇷 France vs. 🇸🇪 Sweden — June 30, 5 p.m. ET, MetLife Stadium, East Rutherford, New Jersey
🇺🇸 United States vs. 🇧🇦 Bosnia and Herzegovina — July 1, 8 p.m. ET, Levi's Stadium, Santa Clara, California
🇦🇺 Australia vs. 🇪🇬 Egypt — July 3, 2 p.m. ET, AT&T Stadium, Arlington, Texas
🇦🇷 Argentina vs. 🇨🇻 Cape Verde — July 3, 6 p.m. ET, Hard Rock Stadium, Miami Gardens, Florida
1:38 a.m. ET — Egypt Saved by VAR in 1-1 Draw With Iran
Egypt and Iran played to a 1-1 draw in Seattle (Photo by Stu Forster/Getty Images)
Egypt and Iran played to a 1-1 draw in their final Group G match in Seattle, a result that confirmed Egypt's place in the round of 32 behind group winners Belgium.
Mahmoud Saber opened the scoring in the fifth minute, the fastest goal in Egypt's World Cup history, before Iran's Mehdi Taremi had his penalty saved and Ramin Rezaeian leveled the match in the 14th minute.
The drama returned late, with Taremi striking the crossbar in the 89th minute before Iran appeared to snatch a stoppage-time winner through Shoja Khalilzadeh, only for the goal to be overturned on VAR review for offside. Saeid Ezatolahi nearly found a winner of his own in the final seconds, but his effort also came back off the crossbar.
Egypt move on to face Australia in the round of 32, while Iran finish third in Group G with three draws and must wait for the rest of the tournament's group-stage results to learn if they qualify as one of the best third-placed teams.
1:15 a.m. ET — Belgium Smash New Zealand to Top Group G
Belgium beat New Zealand 5-1 behind a Leandro Trossard brace (Photo by Alex Grimm/Getty Images)
Belgium closed out the Group G stage with a 5-1 win over New Zealand in Vancouver, finishing as group winners after Egypt's 1-1 draw with Iran in the simultaneous fixture.
Leandro Trossard scored twice in the first 50 minutes, sandwiched around an overturned penalty decision, before Kevin De Bruyne made it 3-0 in the 66th minute with a low drive from outside the box.
New Zealand pulled a goal back through Elijah Just in the 84th minute, but Belgium's bench settled the contest almost immediately: substitute Romelu Lukaku headed in a cross from fellow substitute Nicolas Raskin just 58 seconds after entering the match, before Alexis Saelemaekers added a fifth in stoppage time.
The result eliminates New Zealand from the tournament, while Egypt advance as runners-up behind Belgium to round out the Group G standings.
Group G
Team P GD Pts
🇧🇪 Belgium 3 +4 5
🇪🇬 Egypt 3 +2 5
🇮🇷 Iran 3 0 3
🇳🇿 New Zealand 3 -6 1
10:40 p.m. ET — Debutants Cape Verde Reach Knockout Stages After Saudi Arabia Draw
Cape Verde reached the knockout stages with a draw against Saudi Arabia (Photo by Francois Nel/Getty Images)
Cape Verde secured a place in the World Cup knockout stage on their tournament debut after a 0-0 draw with Saudi Arabia in Houston, finishing second in Group H behind Spain.
The result was confirmed once Spain's 1-0 win over Uruguay in the group's other fixture meant a draw was enough for Cape Verde to advance regardless of the outcome in Houston.
Cape Verde came closest to a winner in the closing seconds, with substitute Nuno da Costa firing wide of an open net after being set up by Garry Rodrigues, while goalkeeper Vozinha and Al Owais each made key saves to keep the match level.
Saudi Arabia, who needed a victory to stay alive, finish bottom of Group H with two points and are eliminated, while Cape Verde complete the group stage unbeaten through three draws and will face defending champions Argentina in the round of 32.
10:22 p.m. ET — Spain Eliminate Uruguay From World Cup
Alex Baena scored the winner as Spain eliminated Uruguay (Photo by David Ramos/Getty Images)
Alex Baena scored the only goal of the match as Spain defeated Uruguay 1-0 in Guadalajara, with Uruguay's elimination confirmed after Cape Verde drew with Saudi Arabia in the group's other fixture.
Baena's 42nd-minute strike came after Marcos Llorente's low cross was not cleared, squeezing past goalkeeper Fernando Muslera, who was substituted at halftime following his third tournament error leading to a goal.
Uruguay also lost midfielder Manuel Ugarte to a stretcher in the 44th minute, and the match turned chaotic in stoppage time when Agustin Canobbio was sent off for a reckless tackle on Pau Cubarsi and contact with the referee.
Spain finish atop Group H, while Cape Verde advance as runners-up. Uruguay's tournament ends after two draws and a loss in the group stage.
Group H
Team P GD Pts
🇪🇸 Spain 3 +5 7
🇨🇻 Cape Verde 3 0 3
🇺🇾 Uruguay 3 -1 2
🇸🇦 Saudi Arabia 3 -4 2
5:45 p.m. ET — Senegal's Five-Goal Display Keeps Knockout Hopes Alive
Senegal hammered Iraq 5-0 to keep their Round of 32 hopes alive (Photo by Robert Cianflone/Getty Images)
Pape Gueye scored twice within 12 minutes of coming on as a substitute as Senegal routed Iraq 5-0 in their final Group I match in Toronto.
Habib Diarra opened the scoring in the third minute, the earliest goal in Senegal's World Cup history, before Iraq's Rebin Sulaka was sent off in the 13th minute for denying a clear goal-scoring opportunity.
Ismaila Sarr added a second goal early in the second half, and Gueye then scored twice in quick succession after replacing Diarra, curling in his first effort just 89 seconds after entering the match.
Iliman Ndiaye capped the scoring in the 82nd minute with a strike from the edge of the area, marking the first time an African nation has scored five goals in a single World Cup match.
Senegal finish third in Group I with the win, while Iraq are eliminated from the tournament without a point from their three matches. The Senegalese still have a chance at making the Round of 32 due to their superior goal difference, but it depends on how results unfold elsewhere.
5:15 p.m. ET — France Run Riot on Norway Behind Dembele Hat-Trick
Ousmane Dembele scored a hat-trick for France against Norway (Photo by Justin Setterfield/Getty Images)
Ousmane Dembele scored three goals in the first half as France defeated Norway 4-1 in Boston, finishing the group stage with a perfect record.
Mike Maignan saved a penalty from Jorgen Strand Larsen in the 50th minute, becoming the first French goalkeeper to stop a World Cup penalty in regulation time since 1986.
Thelo Aasgaard pulled a goal back for Norway in the 21st minute, but Dembele's third goal in the 32nd minute restored France's two-goal cushion before Desire Doue added a stoppage-time header to complete the scoring.
Norway made 10 changes to their starting lineup from their previous match, with Erling Haaland left among the substitutes and unused.
Both teams advance to the round of 32, with France set to face Sweden as Group I winners and Norway moving on to meet Ivory Coast.
Group I
Team P GD Pts
🇫🇷 France 3 +8 9
🇳🇴 Norway 3 +1 6
🇸🇳 Senegal 3 +2 3
🇮🇶 Iraq 3 -11 0
4:30 p.m. ET — Pochettino in Talks Over New Deal With USMNT
USMNT boss Mauricio Pochettino (Photo by Soobum Im/Getty Images)
U.S. Soccer is in advanced discussions to extend Mauricio Pochettino’s contract as head coach of the USMNT through the 2030 FIFA World Cup, sources told ESPN. The proposed deal would keep the Argentine manager in charge for another four-year cycle following the conclusion of the 2026 tournament, with the States also hoping to maintain the current core of players into the next World Cup.
Pochettino has yet to make a final decision and is expected to focus solely on the USMNT’s World Cup campaign before addressing his future. The ex-PSG, Chelsea, and Spurs boss has reportedly attracted interest from top European clubs, but sources say he has been impressed by the project being built within the USMNT.
The contract talks come after an impressive group-stage showing at the 2026 World Cup, where the United States finished atop Group D despite a 3-2 defeat to Türkiye in its final match. The Americans will now lock horns with Bosnia and Herzegovina in the Round of 32 as they look to make a deep run on home soil.

🇳🇿 New Zealand vs 🇧🇪 Belgium: Knockout Places Still Up for Grabs
11:00 PM ET, Vancouver
New Zealand and Belgium enter their final Group G match in Vancouver with both teams still capable of reaching the Round of 32.
New Zealand remain winless in eight World Cup matches after surrendering a half-time lead in a 3-1 defeat to Egypt. A first victory at the tournament could still lift Darren Bazeley’s team into the knockout stage, depending on the result between Egypt and Iran.
Belgium sit third after drawing their opening two games, including a goalless result against Iran. The Red Devils are under pressure to avoid a second consecutive group-stage elimination, although a draw may be enough to secure qualification as one of the best third-placed teams.
Chris Wood will be New Zealand’s main attacking threat, while Thibaut Courtois could become Belgium’s most-capped World Cup player. The goalkeeper has recorded eight clean sheets at the tournament.
Belgium will be without suspended defender Nathan Ngoy following his red card against Iran, but Jérémy Doku is expected to return.

🇪🇬 Egypt vs 🇮🇷 Iran: Group G Qualification at Stake in Seattle
11:00 PM ET, Seattle
Egypt can secure first place in Group G with victory over Iran, while the Persian Cheetahs are chasing their first appearance in the World Cup knockout stage.
The Pharaohs moved to four points after coming from behind to beat New Zealand 3-1, earning their first World Cup victory in four tournament appearances. A win will guarantee top spot, while Egypt could still progress even with a defeat.
Iran remain unbeaten after following a 2-2 draw with New Zealand with a goalless result against Belgium. Victory would send Amir Ghalenoei’s team into the Round of 32, while a draw may leave them waiting on results elsewhere.
Mostafa Ziko has contributed three goals and one assist across Egypt’s last four matches. Mehdi Taremi remains Iran’s main attacking threat after having a goal disallowed against Belgium.
Hamdi Fathy and Hossam Abdelmaguid are injury doubts for Egypt, while Iran have reported no fresh absentees.

🇺🇾 Uruguay vs 🇪🇸 Spain: La Celeste Fight to Avoid Early Exit
8:00 PM ET, Zapopan
Uruguay need a positive result against Spain in Guadalajara to avoid the risk of a second consecutive group-stage elimination.
La Celeste have drawn both of their Group H matches, finishing 1-1 against Saudi Arabia before a 2-2 draw with Cape Verde. They remain in control of their qualification hopes and could even finish first with a victory, depending on the result of Cape Verde’s match against Saudi Arabia.
Spain followed a goalless opening draw against Cape Verde with a convincing 4-0 victory over Saudi Arabia. Luis de la Fuente’s team need a win to guarantee top spot, although a draw is likely to be enough.
Spain are unbeaten in all 10 previous meetings with Uruguay, including draws at the 1950 and 1990 World Cups.
Lamine Yamal could become only the second teenager after Pelé in 1958 to score in consecutive World Cup matches. Uruguay will be without injured defender Ronald Araújo and midfielder Giorgian de Arrascaeta.

🇨🇻 Cape Verde vs 🇸🇦 Saudi Arabia: Knockout Hopes on the Line in Houston
8:00 PM ET, Houston
Cape Verde and Saudi Arabia enter their final Group H match with a chance of reaching the knockout stage.
Cape Verde remain unbeaten after earning draws against Spain and Uruguay. Another point could be enough to send the World Cup debutants through as one of the best third-placed teams, while victory could lift them into the top two.
Bubista’s side have lost only once inside 90 minutes across their last 17 matches. However, defender Sidny Lopes Cabral will miss the game through suspension.
Saudi Arabia sit bottom of the group after drawing with Uruguay and losing 4-0 to Spain, but remain only one point behind second place. A victory is essential to preserve their qualification hopes.
Saudi Arabia faced 49 shots across their opening two matches while attempting only 10, underlining the pressure they have absorbed defensively.

🇸🇳 Senegal vs 🇮🇶 Iraq: Both Teams Chase First World Cup Win
3:00 PM ET, Toronto
Senegal and Iraq will try to keep their knockout hopes alive when they complete their Group I campaigns in Toronto.
Both teams have lost their opening two matches, although a victory could still give the winner a chance of advancing as one of the best third-placed sides.
Senegal suffered defeats against France and Norway, conceding three goals in each game. Ismaïla Sarr scored twice in the 3-2 loss to Norway and will again be one of their main attacking threats.
Iraq followed a 4-1 defeat to Norway with a 3-0 loss against France. Their poor goal difference means even a victory may not be enough to secure qualification.
Goalkeeper Édouard Mendy is a doubt for Senegal after leaving the Norway match injured, while Iraq forward Aymen Hussein was withdrawn against France.

🇳🇴 Norway vs 🇫🇷 France: Group I Leaders Meet in Foxborough
3:00 PM ET, Foxborough
Norway and France have already secured places in the knockout stage, but top spot in Group I remains at stake when they meet in Foxborough, Massachusetts.
Norway have won their opening two matches, defeating Iraq 4-1 and Senegal 3-2, but trail France on goal difference and must win to finish first. Erling Haaland has scored twice in each game and enters the match level with Kylian Mbappé on four tournament goals.
France have scored six goals across victories over Senegal and Iraq. Mbappé now has 16 goals in 16 World Cup appearances, leaving him two behind Lionel Messi’s competition record of 18.
France need only a draw to win the group, while Norway are looking for their first World Cup victory against European opposition.
Julian Ryerson is a doubt for Norway after leaving their previous match with an injury. France have close to a full-strength squad available.

6:00 a.m. ET — June 26 World Cup Schedule: France, Spain and Belgium Complete Group Stage
Six matches are scheduled for June 26 as Groups G, H and I complete their schedules at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
France face Norway in Foxborough, while Senegal meet Iraq in Toronto in the two early Group I matches. Spain then play Uruguay in Zapopan, with Cape Verde facing Saudi Arabia in Houston later in the evening.
The day concludes with Belgium taking on New Zealand in Vancouver and Egypt meeting Iran in Seattle, with qualification still undecided in Group G.
Four Round of 32 matchups were confirmed following Thursday’s results. Canada will face South Africa, Brazil will meet Japan, the Netherlands will play Morocco and the United States will take on Bosnia and Herzegovina.

Sergey Demidov is a veteran sports journalist with over 18 years of experience covering international sports. Since beginning his career in 2007, he has reported on the Olympic Games (Sochi 2014), FIFA World Cup (2014), UEFA Euro (2012), World Cup of Hockey (2016), and Stanley Cup Finals (since 2017). Before joining RG, Sergey was an independent senior editor at NHL.com/ru (2016–present) and contributed to Gazeta.ru and TASS News Agency. Currently, he leads RG’s global news desk, overseeing correspondents across North America, Europe, Africa, Asia, and South America. He has conducted one-on-one interviews with elite athletes, including Jaromir Jagr and Alex Ovechkin, former WADA and FIFA presidents Craig Reedie and Sepp Blatter, and soccer coaches like Fabio Capello. Sergey’s editorial work emphasizes accuracy, sourcing transparency, and a neutral voice, aligned with the Trust Project and Google Publisher Center principles. He is bilingual in English and Russian, specializing in NHL hockey. 📍 Based in: Pafos, Cyprus 🌐 Languages: English, Russian, Greek
